mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the unemployment rate signal improving labor market conditions that boost consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC over 2-year windows is below 0.03 or the proportion of positive 20-day returns following UNRATE decreases is not significantly above 65.5% baseline (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 2.5%, it signals the start of an easing cycle that lowers discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Less than 60% of 20-day forward returns are positive in the 3 months following FEDFUNDS crossing below 2.5%, or the mean return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and month-over-month growth in housing starts signals robust domestic demand that drives corporate earnings and stock prices higher over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 and HOUST increase, the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of such episodes or the mean return is not positive with statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (industrial production YoY growth exceeding 2%), rising INDPRO reflects strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: When INDPRO YoY is above 2%, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the IC of INDPRO growth versus forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising consumer price index erodes real purchasing power and prompts expectations of monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly change and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is non-negative over any 5-year window, or the mean forward return after CPIAUCSL increases is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales signals strong consumer-driven economic expansion that boosts corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase month-over-month, the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, which reduces earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses above 6%, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of episodes, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS above 3%), continued strong GDP growth signals a resilient economy that can withstand higher rates, sustaining corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening with strong economic growth
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the rank IC for the interaction term is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.
